actions: searched architect and coder prompts for skill/knowledge/context dispatch references
read system-enhancer coder context pack and role-filter

summary: opencode-swarm-main has no first-class skill loader; its analogue is a two-tier knowledge system with project-local swarm JSONL and platform-level hive shared-learnings JSONL, controlled by knowledge config.
Automatic phase-start knowledge injection targets only architect/orchestrator agents, using chat message transform hooks and an explicit architect allowlist after stripping swarm prefixes.

Architect injection is compact and ranked: it reads merged swarm+hive knowledge, budgets lessons/run memory/drift/rejected warnings, dedupes, filters by scope, excludes archived entries, and scores by category/confidence/keywords.

Coder workers have an automatic context pack in system-enhancer: when baseRole is coder, it uses declaredCoderScope[0] as a knowledge_recall query and injects a CONTEXT FROM KNOWLEDGE BASE block if results exist.
The coder context pack can also inject prior reviewer rejections and repo-graph localization for the declared primary file, making worker context hook-injected, role-specific, and scope-keyed.

Architect dispatch still explicitly calls knowledge_recall for hidden coupling before coder delegation, then expands AFFECTS with blast-radius notes; worker auto-loading does not replace orchestrator-mediated context selection.
DelegationEnvelope.technicalContext is only a string, and the coder prompt expects plain INPUT requirements/context; there is no typed skills/capability/context-pack field in the dispatch envelope.

Role-filter supports [FOR: ...] targeted context, but system prompts, delegation envelopes, plan content, and knowledge content are never filtered; /swarm knowledge commands manage stores rather than auto-loading worker skills.
